body:
## Critical: Rust `--capture browser` always times out on Google Docs

It is unacceptable that `--capture browser` does not work in the Rust CLI. This is the primary feature differentiator from `--capture api`, and when a user explicitly requests browser capture, it must work — not silently fall back to a degraded path.

### Root cause

The Rust browser capture uses Chrome's `--dump-dom` mode, which waits for `DOMContentLoaded`. Google Docs is a SPA that loads its content asynchronously via JavaScript after the initial page load, so `--dump-dom` times out every time:

```
DEBUG launching headless Chrome for DOM capture
  args=["--headless=new", "--dump-dom", "--timeout=30000",
        "--virtual-time-budget=8000", ...]
WARN  real-browser Google Docs capture failed; falling back to editor HTTP fetch
  error=Browser error: Timed out waiting for headless Chrome to render
```

The `--dump-dom` approach fundamentally cannot work for SPAs like Google Docs. The fix should use CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol) to:
1. Navigate to the page
2. Wait for `DOCS_modelChunk` to appear in the JS context
3. Extract the model data via `Runtime.evaluate`

This is exactly what the JS version does successfully with Playwright. The Rust version has `browser-commander` as a dependency which supports CDP — it should use it.

### Full debug log

```
DEBUG starting capture url=.../edit format=archive capture=browser
DEBUG selected Google Docs capture method method=BrowserModel
DEBUG capturing Google Doc editor model with a real browser
INFO  Rendering HTML for URL: .../edit
DEBUG launching headless Chrome for DOM capture
  chrome=/Applications/Google Chrome.app/Contents/MacOS/Google Chrome
  args=["--headless=new", "--disable-gpu", "--disable-extensions",
        "--disable-dev-shm-usage", "--dump-dom", "--timeout=30000",
        "--virtual-time-budget=8000", "--run-all-compositor-stages-before-draw",
        "--window-size=1280,800", ".../edit"]
WARN  real-browser Google Docs capture failed; falling back to editor HTTP fetch
  error=Browser error: Timed out waiting for headless Chrome to render .../edit
INFO  Fetching HTML from URL: .../edit
INFO  Successfully fetched HTML (432462 bytes)
DEBUG fetched Google Docs editor HTML through HTTP fallback
DEBUG extracted Google Docs editor model chunks chunks=2
INFO  parsed Google Docs editor model chunks=2 cid_urls=4 blocks=121 tables=5 images=26 text_bytes=3323
```

The fallback to HTTP fetch works and extracts the model, but this means `--capture browser` never actually uses a browser for Google Docs. The HTTP fallback should be the `--capture api` path — browser capture must use a real browser or report a clear error.

## Tables have extra empty columns (both Rust and JS browser)

All tables are rendered with ghost empty columns between real columns:

**Expected (3 columns):**
```markdown
| Feature | Supported | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Bold | Yes | Using double asterisks |
```

**Actual (5 columns):**
```markdown
| Feature |  | Supported |  | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Bold |  | Yes |  | Using double asterisks |
```

This affects all tables in the document. The root cause needs to be found and fixed — likely the `DOCS_modelChunk` table parser is inserting an extra cell boundary between each real cell.

## Rust browser: images not downloaded (0/4)

The Rust browser capture extracts `docs-images-rt` URLs correctly but does not download them to the `images/` directory in archive mode:

```markdown
![Blue rectangle](https://docs.google.com/docs-images-rt/ABaEjg1y9qE4...=s2048)
```

The JS version downloads these to `images/image-01.png` etc. The Rust version should do the same in archive mode.

## Nested ordered sub-lists rendered as bullets (JS) or wrong nesting (both)

**Source:**
```markdown
1.  Parent item 1
    1.  Child item 1.1
    2.  Child item 1.2
```

**JS browser output** (numbered sub-items become bullets):
```markdown
1. Parent item 1
  - Child item 1.1
  - Child item 1.2
```

**Rust browser output** (correct numbers but nesting flattened):
```markdown
1. Parent item 1
  1. Child item 1.1
  2. Child item 1.2
```

Both need to preserve the original ordered list structure with proper nesting.

## Empty table cell positions shifted

**Source:**
```markdown
| A | B | C |
|---|---|---|
|   | x |   |
| y |   | z |
```

**Actual (cells in wrong columns):**
```markdown
| A |  | B |  | C |
|  | x |  |  |  |
| y |  |  | z |  |
```

The `x` should be in column B, but it appears in a ghost column. Combined with the extra-columns bug, cell positions become unpredictable.

## Nested list extra blank lines (both)

Blank lines inserted between nested list children, breaking list continuity:

```markdown
- Parent item 1

  - Child item 1a

  - Child item 1b
```

Should be:
```markdown
- Parent item 1
  - Child item 1a
  - Child item 1b
```

## Summary of all remaining issues

| # | Issue | Rust | JS | Priority |
|---|-------|------|-----|----------|
| 1 | **Browser capture times out on Google Docs** — uses `--dump-dom` instead of CDP | ❌ Always fails | ✅ Works | **Critical** |
| 2 | **Tables have ghost empty columns** | ❌ | ❌ | High |
| 3 | **Images not downloaded in archive mode** | ❌ 0/4 | ✅ 4/4 | High |
| 4 | **Nested ordered sub-lists** wrong type or nesting | Partial | ❌ bullets | Medium |
| 5 | **Empty table cell positions shifted** | ❌ | ❌ | Medium |
| 6 | **Extra blank lines in nested lists** | ❌ | ❌ | Low |

All of these need root causes identified and fixed. The test document at https://docs.google.com/document/d/1f5zI2xOFpKa90v0GjamO_t7lqSdzMlaM/edit and reference markdown at https://gist.github.com/konard/f9533797242666c03097440fd3e30ad5 should be used to validate each fix.

## Related issues

- #90 — Reference test document
- #92 — Previous remaining issues (partially addressed)
- #96 — Previous version test results
- #80 — Original Rust browser capture issue

## Applies to

- [x] Rust CLI (v0.3.4)
- [x] JavaScript CLI (v1.7.12)

comments.body:
We need to download all logs and data related about the issue to this repository, make sure we compile that data to `./docs/case-studies/issue-{id}` folder, and use it to do deep case study analysis (also make sure to search online for additional facts and data), in which we will reconstruct timeline/sequence of events, list of each and all requirements from the issue, find root causes of the each problem, and propose possible solutions and solution plans for each requirement (we should also check known existing components/libraries, that solve similar problem or can help in solutions).

If there is not enough data to find actual root cause, add debug output and verbose mode if not present, that will allow us to find root cause on next iteration.

If issue related to any other repository/project, where we can report issues on GitHub, please do so. Each issue must contain reproducible examples, workarounds and suggestions for fix the issue in code.

title: v0.3.4 / v1.7.12: Rust browser capture always times out on Google Docs, tables have ghost columns, images not downloaded
